resources.string: HyTalkPTT is OFF in Settings → Accessibility. Enable it for side/programmable PTT keys. If Bluetooth PTT still shows nothing below, your mic may use a non-standard protocol (e.g. SPP only) that Android does not expose as keys.

Press the device side PTT key (shown here via keyboard events). Bluetooth: only works if the phone delivers headset or media keys to apps (HFP hook / AVRCP). Many dedicated PTT mics (e.g. some Inrico models) use a private protocol — then nothing appears in logcat (tag HyTalkPTT-MediaBtn) and you must use the device’s own PTT key only.
Bluetooth headset / microphone PTT (HFP hook, AVRCP play/pause)
Important: enable “Bluetooth headset / microphone PTT” above and tap Save. Until you Save, headset keys are not registered when you leave this screen (see log: Bluetooth PTT OFF).

Huawei often sends headset PLAY/PAUSE to com.android.mediacenter (Huawei Music), not to other apps — system log: “Sending KeyEvent … to com.android.mediacenter”. Force-stop or disable Huawei Music / Media center, enable Bluetooth PTT + Save, then try again. Dedicated PTT on some mics never appears in system logs — use the phone side key. Diagnose: adb logcat | grep -i MediaSessionService
AVRCP Play (MEDIA_PLAY) triggers PTT — uncheck if the headset power / hook button falsely keys PTT (same key code as many “play” buttons).
Enable at least one PTT source

Scroll screen…

Other accessibility services may interfere with global key capture. For reliable PTT, enable only HyTalkPTT under Settings → Accessibility (turn off any others you do not need).

To use HyTalkPTT, do this in order:

1) Accessibility: tap Configure Accessibility — Settings → Accessibility → HyTalkPTT → enable the toggle
2) Configure PTT Key: tap Configure PTT Key — press your PTT button, enable Bluetooth PTT if needed, then Save settings
3) Configure Programmable Keys (for Motorola): Settings → Programmable Keys → Select PTT Key app → HyTalkPTT
